Description: Represents a security code input component that allows users to enter a code based on a specified pattern.

Parameters.Name: Pattern
Parameters.Description:
The pattern of the security code. e.g. ("##/##/19##" would represent "MM/DD/YYYY" where the first two YY are filled in.). Default Placeholders (can be changed)>. # is a placeholder for a numeric value. A is a placeholder for an alphabetic character. ? is a placeholder for an alphabetic or numeric character. @ is a placeholder for a special symbol. * is a placeholder for any UTF-8 character.
Remarks: Defaults to "####"

Parameters.Name: PlaceholderNumeric
Parameters.Description:
The placeholder character for numeric values.
Remarks: Defaults to '#', translates to char.IsDigit

Parameters.Name: PlaceholderAlpha
Parameters.Description:
The placeholder character for alphabetic characters.
Remarks: Defaults to 'A', translates to char.IsLetter

Parameters.Name: PlaceholderAlphaNumeric
Parameters.Description:
The placeholder character for alphabetic or numeric characters.
Remarks: Defaults to '?', translates to char.IsLetterOrDigit

Parameters.Name: PlaceholderSpecial
Parameters.Description:
The placeholder character for special characters, typically symbols.
Remarks: Defaults to '@', translates to char.IsSymbol or char.IsPunctuation

Parameters.Name: PlaceholderAny
Parameters.Description:
The placeholder character for any UTF-8 character.
Remarks: Defaults to '*', translates to any UTF-8 char.

Parameters.Name: Code
Parameters.Description:
The current value of the security code.
Remarks: Defaults to null.

Parameters.Name: CodeChanged
Parameters.Description: Called when the value of the security code changes.

Parameters.Name: Password
Parameters.Description:
If true, each input will be masked as a password.
Remarks: Defaults to false.

Parameters.Name: Spacing
Parameters.Description:
The gap between items in increments of 4px.
Remarks: Defaults to 3, maximum of 20 (80px)

Parameters.Name: Horizontal
Parameters.Description:
Whether to display items horizontally, if true will display items horizontally, false will display items vertically
Remarks: Defaults to true.

Parameters.Name: Variant
Parameters.Description:
The display variant of the component. Options are Outlined, Text, and Filled.
Remarks: Defaults to Outlined

Parameters.Name: ReadOnlyVariant
Parameters.Description:
The display variant of the code when readonly. Options are Outlined, Text, and Filled.
Remarks: Defaults to Text

Parameters.Name: Underline
Parameters.Description:
If true, the underline will be visible.
Remarks: Defaults to true.

Parameters.Name: Margin
Parameters.Description:
The margin of the component.
Remarks: Defaults to Normal

Parameters.Name: Class
Parameters.Description:
The CSS classes applied to this component.
Remarks: Defaults to null. You can use spaces to separate multiple classes. Use the Style property to apply custom CSS styles.

Parameters.Name: Style
Parameters.Description:
The CSS styles applied to this component.
Remarks: Defaults to null. Use the Class property to apply CSS classes.

Parameters.Name: Tag
Parameters.Description:
The arbitrary object to link to this component.
Remarks: This property is typically used to associate additional information with this component, such as a model containing data for this component.

Parameters.Name: UserAttributes
Parameters.Description:
The additional HTML attributes to apply to this component.
Remarks: This property is typically used to provide additional HTML attributes during rendering such as ARIA accessibility tags or a custom ID.

Methods.Name: ClipboardPasteEvent
Methods.Description:
Handles the clipboard paste event triggered in javascript by processing the pasted text and updating the corresponding code items.
Remarks: This method processes the pasted text by matching it against the editable and fixed characters in the code items. Editable code items are updated with valid characters from the pasted text, while fixed code items are set to their predefined values. After processing, the method updates the code value, moves focus to the next focusable item, and validates the form if applicable.
